title: Volutaria belouinii
taxonomy: Volutaria belouinii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Volutaria, which includes a few species of annual plants. The plant is native to North Africa, specifically in Morocco. Volutaria belouinii is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive flowers and foliage. Further classification details include its placement in the subtribe Centauroideae.
uses: Volutaria belouinii has been traditionally used in North African folk medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its extracts have been used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Research suggests that Volutaria belouinii may have antioxidant and antifungal activities. However, more studies are needed to fully understand its medicinal applications.

intro: Volutaria belouinii, also known as Belouin's thistle, is a plant species native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region, belonging to the family Compositae (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Volutaria belouinii include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Volutaria belouinii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
